sourceMovie.title: Showgirls
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Showgirls works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Black Swan
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Showgirls clicked for you, Black Swan (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Gone Baby Gone
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Showgirls clicked for you, Gone Baby Gone (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Darjeeling Limited
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Showgirls clicked for you, The Darjeeling Limited (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Showgirls?
faq.answer: Try Black Swan, Gone Baby Gone, The Darjeeling Limited—each shares DNA with Showgirls while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Showgirls?
faq.answer: Showgirls (1995) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Showgirls part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Showgirls Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
